channel.title: Book on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Book on Alien Truth Social

item.title: They Knew Too Much About Flying Saucers
item.description:
They Knew Too Much About Flying Saucers is a 1956 book by Gray Barker that holds historical significance as the first text to introduce the “Men in Black” trope into UFO conspiracy culture. Barker, a member of the International Flying Saucer Bureau, alleged that researchers like Albert K. Bender were being intimidated and silenced by mysterious figures to prevent the disclosure of extraterrestrial truths. While the book became a bestseller and influenced decades of paranormal folklore, later investigations—including comments by collaborator John Sherwood—suggest that Barker significantly embellished or fabricated these accounts to capitalize on public interest. The work ultimately shaped the foundational imagery for modern pop culture franchises, including The X-Files and the Men in Black film series.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/They_Knew_Too_Much_About_Flying_Saucers

item.title: The Flying Saucers Are Real
item.description:
Donald Keyhoe’s 1950 book, The Flying Saucers Are Real, investigated United States Air Force reports of UFOs between 1947 and 1950. The book, which followed a similar article published in True magazine, popularized many ideas in ufology. Keyhoe argued that the Air Force had a policy of concealing UFO existence until 1949, after which it shifted to gradual revelation. He also contended that Earth had been visited by extraterrestrials for two centuries, with increased activity after the 1945 atomic bomb test.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Flying_Saucers_Are_Real

item.title: Area 51: The Dreamland Chronicles
item.description:
Area 51: The Dreamland Chronicles is a 1997 non-fiction book by David Darlington about Area 51 in Nevada. Reviews noted the book’s service in detailing the site’s early history while also criticizing its inclusion of accounts from “alien seekers” and “saucer nuts.” The book has been called a definitive work on Area 51 and its cultural impact, with one review describing it as even-handed towards ufologists. It was published by Henry Holt & Company.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Area_51:_The_Dreamland_Chronicles

item.title: New Lands
item.description:
New Lands is the second nonfiction book by Charles Fort, published in 1923 by Boni & Liveright. The book primarily addresses astronomical anomalies and is described as a satirical critique of astronomers. Fort criticizes astronomical predictions and scientific dogma, expanding on his theories of a “Super-Sargasso Sea” and continents above Earth. Despite its content, New Lands is considered Fort’s worst-regarded book, with its speculations on sky continents and Earth’s shape being seen as dated. The book had poor sales, leading to Boni & Liveright declining to publish Fort’s subsequent work.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_Lands

item.title: Flying Saucers from Outer Space
item.description:
Flying Saucers from Outer Space is a non-fiction book about UFOs written by Donald Keyhoe and published on October 5, 1953. The book was followed by The Flying Saucer Conspiracy in 1955 and preceded by The Flying Saucers Are Real in 1950. In 1956, a science-fiction film titled Earth vs. the Flying Saucers was made, suggested by Keyhoe’s book.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flying_Saucers_from_Outer_Space

item.title: The Coming of the Saucers
item.description:
The Coming of the Saucers is a 1952 book by Kenneth Arnold and Raymond Palmer that expands on their earlier articles about unidentified flying objects. The book includes Arnold’s firsthand accounts of his sighting of flying disc-like shapes and his investigations into various UFO incidents. It also features third-person summaries of other UFO reports and is noted for introducing the concept of the “man in black.” The work has been cited as fueling the extraterrestrial hypothesis and influencing later UFO conspiracy theories.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Coming_of_the_Saucers
